name: Alumahan
description: A common, medium-sized mackerel that swims in schools and is important for both local consumption and commercial fisheries.
location: Coastal waters of Ballesteros (Cagayan) and Ivana (Batanes)

name: Angelfish
description: Colorful marine angelfish known for their flattened bodies and graceful movements.
location: Coral gardens near Piat (Cagayan) and Basco’s reef zones (Batanes)

name: Flying Fish
description: Fish with enlarged pectoral fins that allow them to glide above the water’s surface.
location: Coastal waters off Pamplona (Cagayan) and offshore Basco (Batanes)

name: Mahi Mahi
description: A fast-growing and colorful pelagic fish found in tropical and subtropical surface waters.
location: Offshore waters around Palaui Island (Cagayan) and Sabtang Channel (Batanes)

name: Grouper
description: Reef-associated fish, highly valued for their culinary qualities..
location: Coral reefs around Sabtang Island (Batanes)

name: Blue Marlin
description: A large and majestic billfish, a prized game fish found in the open ocean.
location: Deep oceanic waters east of Sta. Ana (Cagayan) and southeast of Basco (Batanes)

name: Maya-Maya
description: Commercially important reef fish with firm, flavorful flesh. Known locally as Maya-Maya and found in reefs off the coast.
location: Coastal areas and reefs of Central Luzon (e.g., Zambales, Bataan)

name: Parrotfish
description: Herbivorous fish with beak-like mouths, important for controlling algae on reefs.
location: Coral reef flats off Abulug (Cagayan) and Diura Fishing Village (Batanes)

name: Sailfish
description: A billfish characterized by its prominent sail-like dorsal fin, inhabiting pelagic waters.
location: Open sea off Gonzaga (Cagayan) and Mavulis Island (Batanes)

name: Butterflyfish
description: Small, often brightly colored reef fish with diverse patterns.
location: Coral-rich zones of Buguey Lagoon (Cagayan) and Marlboro Hills reefs (Batanes)

name: Skipjack Tuna
description: An important pelagic tuna species commonly caught in the waters off Luzon, including the Ilocos coast.
location: Offshore waters of the Ilocos Region

name: Cavalla
description: Active predatory fish often associated with reefs and coastal areas. Also known as Talakitok.
location: Coastal waters of the Ilocos Region

name: Yellowfin Tuna
description: A migratory pelagic tuna species highly valued for its meat. Also known as Bariles (juvenile).
location: Offshore waters accessible from the Ilocos Region

name: Tanguigue
description: A large mackerel species known for its firm texture and rich flavor, inhabiting pelagic waters.
location: Offshore waters of Buguey Bay (Cagayan) and near Itbayat Island (Batanes)

name: President’s Fish
description: Known for its high value and rarity, ludong is a catadromous species that migrates from freshwater to marine environments to spawn.
location: Found in the Cagayan River and its tributaries, particularly in Aparri, Cagayan, and the Addalem River in Quirino.

name: Sea Catfish
description: Endemic sea catfish species abundant in the Cagayan River and its larger tributaries.
location: Commonly found in the Magat River at Bagabag, Nueva Vizcaya.

name: Tilapia
description: Introduced species favored in aquaculture for its fast growth and adaptability.
location: Farmed throughout Cagayan Valley, especially in Isabela and Nueva Vizcaya.

name: Silver Perch
description: Endemic to the Philippines, prized for its delicate taste.
location: Inhabits lakes and rivers across the region.

name: Striped Snakehead
description: Popular for its firm meat; often grilled or used in native dishes.
location: Found in rice fields, swamps, and rivers around Cagayan Valley.

name: Catfish
description: Known for its resilience and is commonly used in adobo dishes.
location: Cultivated in fish farms and found in freshwater bodies in the region.

name: Freshwater Eel
description: Elongated, snake-like fish caught for its rich, flavorful meat.
location: Found in rivers and streams throughout the Cagayan Valley.

name: Snakeskin Gourami
description: Hardy fish found both in aquaculture and in the wild.
location: Inhabits freshwater bodies in the region.

name: Trumpet Snail
description: Often collected for food, commonly found in estuarine and coastal areas.
location: Found in the estuarine and coastal areas of the region.

name: Freshwater Snail
description: Native freshwater snails that are part of the local diet.
location: Common in rivers and streams throughout the Cagayan Valley.

name: Freshwater Clam
description: Freshwater clams that are harvested for their edible meat.
location: Found in sandy and muddy bottoms of rivers in the region.

name: Giant Freshwater Prawn
description: Known for their large size and sweet meat.
location: Cultivated and caught in freshwater bodies across the Cagayan Valley.

name: Common Carp
description: Introduced for aquaculture purposes and known for adaptability.
location: Farmed in ponds and found in rivers throughout the region.

name: Climbing Perch
description: Hardy fish that can survive in low-oxygen waters.
location: Found in ponds, swamps, and slow-moving waters in the Cagayan Valley.

name: Silver Barb
description: Fast-growing species used in aquaculture.
location: Cultivated in fish farms and found in freshwater bodies in the region.

name: Sea Catfish
description: Often used in sour soups like sinigang.
location: Caught in brackish and coastal waters, including those near the Cagayan Valley.

name: Silver Perch
description: Another name for ayungin, prized for its delicate flavor.
location: Inhabits lakes and rivers, including those in the Cagayan Valley.

name: Tank Goby
description: A goby species found in freshwater and brackish environments.
location: Inhabits rivers and estuaries within the Cagayan Valley.

name: Anchovy
description: Often sun-dried and used as toppings or side dishes.
location: Caught in large numbers in the coastal areas of the region.

name: Sardine
description: Small schooling fish, commonly caught and preserved as dried fish.
location: Abundant in the marine waters off the coast of Cagayan.
